title: pseudoselago peninsulae
intro: Pseudoselago peninsulae is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the Peninsula False Sage or Cape Peninsula Heath. The plant has been traditionally used in South African folk medicine, and it has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its antiseptic and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Pseudoselago peninsulae include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Pseudoselago peninsulae is often used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to make teas, syrups, or topical creams, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
